title: Sarah Received the Promised Child
copy: After a long and humanly impossible wait, God fulfilled His word.
whatGodDid: The Lord visited Sarah exactly as He had promised and gave Abraham and Sarah their son Isaac.
outcome: Sarah’s laughter of disbelief became the laughter of testimony.
verses: Is any thing too hard for the Lord?
And the Lord visited Sarah as he had said... and Sarah conceived.
God hath made me to laugh.

title: Hannah’s Prayer Was Answered
copy: She poured out her soul, found peace, and later held Samuel.
need: A child and relief from deep sorrow
whatGodDid: God remembered Hannah and enabled her to conceive.
outcome: She gave birth to Samuel and dedicated him to the Lord.
verses: I am a woman of a sorrowful spirit... but have poured out my soul before the Lord.
Hannah conceived, and bare a son, and called his name Samuel.
My heart rejoiceth in the Lord.

title: Joseph Went from Prison to Palace
copy: God turned years of injustice into preservation and leadership.
need: Deliverance from false accusation and forgotten seasons
whatGodDid: God gave Joseph wisdom to interpret Pharaoh’s dreams and favour to lead Egypt.
outcome: Joseph became governor and later preserved his family during famine.
verses: Then Pharaoh sent and called Joseph, and they brought him hastily out of the dungeon.
See, I have set thee over all the land of Egypt.
God meant it unto good, to bring to pass... to save much people alive.

title: The Red Sea Opened
copy: When Israel was trapped, God made a road through the sea.
whatGodDid: God divided the sea and led Israel across on dry ground.
outcome: The pursuing Egyptian army was defeated and Israel feared and believed the Lord.
verses: Stand still, and see the salvation of the Lord... The Lord shall fight for you.
The Lord caused the sea to go back... and the children of Israel went into the midst of the sea upon the dry ground.
And the people feared the Lord, and believed the Lord.

title: Jericho’s Walls Fell
copy: Israel obeyed an unusual instruction and God brought down the stronghold.
whatGodDid: God gave Joshua a strategy built on obedience, worship, and His power.
outcome: The wall fell flat and Israel entered the city.
verses: See, I have given into thine hand Jericho.
The wall fell down flat, so that the people went up into the city.
By faith the walls of Jericho fell down.

title: Ruth’s Life Was Restored
copy: Widowhood and displacement became provision, family and legacy.
whatGodDid: God led Ruth to Boaz, a kinsman-redeemer who treated her with honour.
outcome: Ruth married Boaz, bore Obed, and entered the lineage of David and Jesus.
verses: A full reward be given thee of the Lord God of Israel, under whose wings thou art come to trust.
The Lord gave her conception, and she bare a son.
They called his name Obed: he is the father of Jesse, the father of David.

title: Esther’s Courage Brought Deliverance
copy: A hidden identity and royal position became tools for rescue.
whatGodDid: God used Esther’s courage, Mordecai’s warning, fasting, and providential timing.
outcome: Haman’s scheme was overturned and the Jews received protection and joy.
verses: Who knoweth whether thou art come to the kingdom for such a time as this?
Let my life be given me at my petition, and my people at my request.
The Jews had light, and gladness, and joy, and honour.

title: Job Was Restored
copy: After severe loss and honest wrestling, God restored him.
need: Strength and restoration after overwhelming suffering
whatGodDid: God revealed His greatness, received Job’s prayer, and turned his captivity.
outcome: The Lord blessed Job’s latter end more than his beginning.
verses: For I know that my redeemer liveth.
The Lord turned the captivity of Job... and gave Job twice as much as he had before.
So the Lord blessed the latter end of Job more than his beginning.

title: God Answered Elijah by Fire
copy: One prophet stood against idolatry and God revealed Himself.
need: A public demonstration of the true God
whatGodDid: God answered Elijah’s prayer with fire that consumed the sacrifice.
outcome: The people fell on their faces and confessed that the Lord is God.
verses: Hear me, O Lord, hear me, that this people may know that thou art the Lord God.
Then the fire of the Lord fell.
The Lord, he is the God; the Lord, he is the God.

title: The Widow’s Oil Multiplied
copy: A small vessel of oil became enough to clear debt and sustain a family.
whatGodDid: God multiplied the widow’s remaining oil as she poured into borrowed vessels.
outcome: She sold the oil, paid her debt, and lived on the rest.
verses: Thine handmaid hath not any thing in the house, save a pot of oil.
And it came to pass, when the vessels were full, that she said unto her son, Bring me yet a vessel.
Pay thy debt, and live thou and thy children of the rest.

title: Naaman Was Healed
copy: Humility and obedience brought cleansing from leprosy.
whatGodDid: God instructed Naaman through Elisha to wash seven times in Jordan.
outcome: His flesh was restored and he confessed the God of Israel.
verses: Go and wash in Jordan seven times, and thy flesh shall come again to thee.
And his flesh came again like unto the flesh of a little child, and he was clean.
Now I know that there is no God in all the earth, but in Israel.

title: The Three Hebrews Survived the Fire
copy: They refused to bow, and God met them inside the furnace.
whatGodDid: God preserved Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego in the fiery furnace.
outcome: They emerged unharmed and the king honoured their God.
verses: Our God whom we serve is able to deliver us... But if not... we will not serve thy gods.
I see four men loose, walking in the midst of the fire, and they have no hurt.
Nor was an hair of their head singed.

title: Daniel Survived the Lions’ Den
copy: His prayer life remained steady and God shut the lions’ mouths.
whatGodDid: God sent His angel and kept the lions from harming Daniel.
outcome: Daniel was lifted out unharmed and the king proclaimed God’s greatness.
verses: He kneeled upon his knees three times a day, and prayed.
My God hath sent his angel, and hath shut the lions' mouths.
No manner of hurt was found upon him, because he believed in his God.

title: Elizabeth Gave Birth to John
copy: A prayer remembered after many years became part of God’s redemption story.
whatGodDid: God heard Zacharias and Elizabeth and gave them John the Baptist.
outcome: Their neighbours rejoiced, and John prepared the way for the Lord.
verses: Thy prayer is heard; and thy wife Elisabeth shall bear thee a son.
Thus hath the Lord dealt with me... to take away my reproach.
Elisabeth... brought forth a son. And her neighbours... rejoiced with her.

title: Jesus Was Born of a Virgin
copy: God fulfilled prophecy and sent His Son into the world.
whatGodDid: The Holy Spirit overshadowed Mary and she conceived Jesus, the Son of God.
outcome: The Saviour was born, and heaven announced peace and great joy.
verses: The Holy Ghost shall come upon thee... therefore also that holy thing which shall be born of thee shall be called the Son of God.
I bring you good tidings of great joy... For unto you is born this day... a Saviour, which is Christ the Lord.
Behold, a virgin shall be with child... and they shall call his name Emmanuel.

title: The Woman with the Issue of Blood Was Healed
copy: Twelve years of suffering ended when she reached for Jesus.
whatGodDid: Jesus responded to her faith and healed her immediately.
outcome: She was made whole and sent away in peace.
verses: If I may touch but his clothes, I shall be whole.
And straightway the fountain of her blood was dried up.
Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace.

title: Blind Bartimaeus Received His Sight
copy: He refused to be silenced and Jesus stopped for him.
whatGodDid: Jesus called Bartimaeus, asked what he wanted, and restored his sight.
outcome: He immediately followed Jesus in the way.
verses: He cried the more a great deal, Thou Son of David, have mercy on me.
Thy faith hath made thee whole. And immediately he received his sight.

title: Lazarus Was Raised from the Dead
copy: Jesus arrived after four days and proved He is the resurrection and the life.
need: Hope in the face of death
whatGodDid: Jesus called Lazarus out of the tomb.
outcome: Lazarus came forth alive and many believed in Jesus.
verses: I am the resurrection, and the life.
And he that was dead came forth... Then many of the Jews... believed on him.

title: Jesus Fed Five Thousand
copy: A small lunch became enough in the hands of Jesus.
need: Food for a large hungry crowd
whatGodDid: Jesus blessed five loaves and two fishes and multiplied them.
outcome: Everyone ate and twelve baskets of fragments remained.
verses: There is a lad here, which hath five barley loaves, and two small fishes.
Jesus took the loaves; and when he had given thanks, he distributed.
And filled twelve baskets with the fragments.

title: Jairus’s Daughter Was Raised
copy: A delayed journey did not prevent Jesus from bringing life.
whatGodDid: Jesus went to Jairus’s home, took the girl by the hand, and commanded her to arise.
outcome: Her spirit returned and she arose immediately.
verses: Fear not: believe only, and she shall be made whole.
And her spirit came again, and she arose straightway.

title: Peter Was Freed from Prison
copy: While the church prayed, an angel opened the prison doors.
whatGodDid: God sent an angel who woke Peter, broke his chains, and led him outside.
outcome: Peter returned to the praying believers and testified of his deliverance.
verses: Peter therefore was kept in prison: but prayer was made without ceasing of the church unto God for him.
And his chains fell off from his hands.
Now I know of a surety, that the Lord hath sent his angel, and hath delivered me.

title: Paul and Silas Were Freed at Midnight
copy: Prayer and praise filled a prison before an earthquake opened every door.
whatGodDid: God sent a great earthquake while Paul and Silas prayed and sang praises.
outcome: The prison doors opened, chains loosened, and the jailer’s household believed.
verses: At midnight Paul and Silas prayed, and sang praises unto God.
Immediately all the doors were opened, and every one's bands were loosed.
And was baptized, he and all his... believing in God with all his house.

title: Saul Encountered Jesus
copy: A persecutor became a chosen witness of Christ.
whatGodDid: The risen Jesus confronted Saul, blinded him temporarily, and sent Ananias to him.
outcome: Saul was baptised, filled with new purpose, and preached that Jesus is the Son of God.
verses: Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me?... I am Jesus whom thou persecutest.
Brother Saul, the Lord... hath sent me, that thou mightest receive thy sight, and be filled with the Holy Ghost.
And straightway he preached Christ in the synagogues, that he is the Son of God.
